items.title: APHM Dismisses ‘Outdated’ PAC Report Amid Reset Initiative
items.summary: APHM says the PAC’s "really outdated" report doesn't take into account what was done in the last 18 months under Reset, adding that it's voluntary for both consumers and providers to participate in Base MHIT. DRG will be limited to Base MHIT too. The post APHM Dismisses ‘Outdated’ PAC Report Amid Reset Initiative appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Medics Seeing Surge In AI-Generated Complaints, Says Indemnity Body
items.summary: These complaint letters can be seven pages, sometimes longer. They are complicated, often quote questionable legal arguments, and some fail to remove the AI’s ‘coaching’ and common tells. The post Medics Seeing Surge In AI-Generated Complaints, Says Indemnity Body appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Interfering With Medical Practice May Be Illegal, MOH Warns TPAs
items.summary: MOH warns MCOs and TPAs that undermining doctors' autonomy in treating patients may breach Act 586, stressing that the main role of payers is to facilitate financial and insurance claims administration, not to regulate the practice of medicine. The post Interfering With Medical Practice May Be Illegal, MOH Warns TPAs appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Sunway Medical Centre Velocity’s A&E Department Provides Timely Care For Heart Attacks, Stroke, And Trauma Cases
items.summary: With emergency physicians and comprehensive in-house facilities such as point-of-care testing, MRI, X-ray, and a procedure room, Sunway Medical Centre Velocity’s A&E department enables prompt and effective emergency care. The post Sunway Medical Centre Velocity’s A&E Department Provides Timely Care For Heart Attacks, Stroke, And Trauma Cases appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Insurers Question ‘Excessive’ Private Health Screenings, Procedures, Hospitalisations
items.summary: CEOs and chairmen of top insurance companies question the need for "excessive" health screenings, tests or procedures, and treatment in private hospitals in Malaysia, especially gastro cases. They also slam itemised billing, comparing hospitals to hotels. The post Insurers Question ‘Excessive’ Private Health Screenings, Procedures, Hospitalisations appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: A Small Pill With Big Responsibility: Why Warfarin Patients Need More Than A Prescription — Noor Hanita Zaini, Nor Zehan Ahmad & Noor Hasliza Che Seman
items.summary: The danger with warfarin is not only the medicine itself, but when patients do not know enough about how to take it safely. The post A Small Pill With Big Responsibility: Why Warfarin Patients Need More Than A Prescription — Noor Hanita Zaini, Nor Zehan Ahmad & Noor Hasliza Che Seman appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Make Free Drinking Water Mandatory In Restaurants And Food Premises
items.summary: Making free drinking water available in restaurants is one of the simplest public health interventions we can implement. It is practical, affordable, and fair. The post Make Free Drinking Water Mandatory In Restaurants And Food Premises appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Will The Real Cause Of Medical Inflation Please Stand Up? — Dr Kamal Amzan
items.summary: When the public sees a hospital bill, it sees the price. It doesn't see what sits behind the price. It doesn't see what is cross-subsidised, which services are undercharged, which costs are bundled, which capabilities must remain available 24 hours a day. The post Will The Real Cause Of Medical Inflation Please Stand Up? — Dr Kamal Amzan appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Governor: Bank Negara Mandated To Protect Insurance Industry
items.summary: BNM governor Abdul Rasheed says Bank Negara protects the insurance and bank industries to maintain stability of the financial sector for continued services to the public, adding that premium increases are needed to ensure health insurance sustainability. The post Governor: Bank Negara Mandated To Protect Insurance Industry appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Genomics Is Coming To Malaysia, But Will It Reach Everyone? — Assoc Prof Dr Teguh Haryo Sasongko
items.summary: The real equity question is not whether genomic technology exists in Malaysia. It is whether a child in a public hospital, a patient in a rural clinic, or a family without savings can benefit from it when it matters. The post Genomics Is Coming To Malaysia, But Will It Reach Everyone? — Assoc Prof Dr Teguh Haryo Sasongko appeared first on CodeBlue .
